title: The Truth

jokes.text: What kind of a sicko, really, would save these... have them in a huge file? Drawers that wide. You know, you just... 'Where the hell is that but...' I mean, is it that hard to get black, round buttons... that they make it into a whole thing?
jokes.setup: Jerry's opening stand-up routine about spare buttons
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of clothing companies creating elaborate systems for something as mundane as replacement buttons
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor about mundane things. Well-executed premise with good escalation but somewhat verbose delivery.

jokes.text: I'd really like to eventually work exclusively... on my papier-mâché hats.
jokes.setup: Patrice discussing her creative aspirations
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous specificity and impracticality of papier-mâché hats as an art form
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the pretentious character through an absurd artistic pursuit. Economic and memorable.

jokes.text: What if it rains? They're art. You hang them on the wall.
jokes.setup: George's practical question about papier-mâché hats
jokes.punchline: Patrice's dismissive response revealing they're not even functional hats
jokes.explanation: Great character dynamic - George's practical nature vs Patrice's pretension. Clean structure.

jokes.text: 'Who so belongs only to his age... reverences only its popinjays and mumbo jumbos.' Thomas Carlyle, 1864. Tommy C.
jokes.setup: Pretentious quote about artistic integrity
jokes.punchline: Calling Thomas Carlyle 'Tommy C' - mixing highbrow with lowbrow casualness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - the casual nickname completely undercuts the pretentious quote. Perfect economy and character integration.

jokes.text: You come barging in here, asking me to contribute money... to a volcano relief fund for Krakatoa. It was supposed to erupt.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining the fraudulent charity situation
jokes.punchline: The absurd premise of a relief fund for a volcano that was 'supposed to erupt'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd premise that fits Kramer's character perfectly. The logic is hilariously flawed.

jokes.text: I don't even pay taxes. Yeah. That's easy when you have no income.
jokes.setup: Jerry's cavalier attitude about taxes
jokes.punchline: George's cutting observation about Jerry's unemployment
jokes.explanation: Perfect quick comeback. George's delivery cuts through Jerry's bravado with surgical precision.

jokes.text: All the little squares have hardened red sauce in them.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about Kramer's pasta-making habits
jokes.punchline: The specific, gross detail about hardened sauce in the strainer squares
jokes.explanation: Good observational detail that's both specific and relatable. The grossness factor adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: It's a riot, Alice.
jokes.setup: Elaine commenting on Kramer dating his roommate being funny
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic Honeymooners reference
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Yeah, Tina likes the couch.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about make-out sessions in the living room
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual, TMI response about his girlfriend's preferences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - obliviously sharing too much information with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: A long, long time ago... in a galaxy far, far away.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking when the charity donation happened
jokes.punchline: Jerry using the Star Wars opening to avoid the topic
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference used for deflection. Somewhat predictable but fits Jerry's character.

jokes.text: Like you'd make this donation for 50 bucks... and I'd start tearing my clothes off?
jokes.setup: Jerry claiming he was thinking of the Krakatoans
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt assessment of Jerry's transparent motives
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Elaine cutting through Jerry's BS with her typical directness.

jokes.text: Those brave Krakatoans... east of Java... who sacrificed so much, for so long.
jokes.setup: Jerry doubling down on his fake concern
jokes.punchline: The overwrought, fake emotional appeal for fictional volcano victims
jokes.explanation: Jerry's commitment to the bit is funny, and the mock-heroic tone works well.

jokes.text: See, that's karma. No, that's Kramer.
jokes.setup: Elaine saying the audit is karma
jokes.punchline: Jerry's correction blaming Kramer instead of karma
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay with economic delivery. The sound similarity makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: And as we speak, at this very moment... he is handing over to her all of my pertinent tax information... and she has assured us... that the matter is well within her field of expertise.
jokes.setup: Jerry's elaborate, formal explanation of his tax solution
jokes.punchline: The overwrought, almost legal-sounding language for a simple favor
jokes.explanation: Jerry's tendency toward elaborate explanations is in character but the bit runs a bit long.

jokes.text: You hate my earrings, don't you? And you didn't comment... on the chopsticks.
jokes.setup: George giving generic breakup lines
jokes.punchline: Patrice's desperate attempts to find specific reasons for the breakup
jokes.explanation: Good character writing - shows Patrice's insecurity and need for validation through specific details.

jokes.text: I want the truth! The truth? You want the truth?!
jokes.setup: Patrice demanding honesty from George
jokes.punchline: The dramatic, almost courtroom-like exchange building to the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic buildup that parodies courtroom dramas while setting up the character assassination to come.

jokes.text: You call everyone by their full name. You called my doorman Sammy, 'Samuel.' But you didn't even say Samuel. You went 'Samuel.'
jokes.setup: George listing Patrice's pretentious behaviors
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about her pretentious pronunciation of 'Samuel'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific observation about pretentious behavior. The pronunciation detail is perfectly observed and relatable.

jokes.text: It's the financial equivalent of a complete rectal examination.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing how horrible an audit is
jokes.punchline: The crude but apt medical comparison
jokes.explanation: Effective visceral comparison that captures the invasive nature of an audit. Crude but earned.

jokes.text: I would've killed this man. Torn him limb from limb. Ripped the flesh right off his bones.
jokes.setup: Jerry's anger about potentially being audited
jokes.punchline: The increasingly violent and graphic threats
jokes.explanation: The escalation works but feels a bit forced. Jerry's character isn't naturally this violent, even in hyperbole.

jokes.text: Here I am, about to go to the electric chair... and my oldest friend is dating the governor.
jokes.setup: Jerry's dire tax situation
jokes.punchline: The prison execution metaphor with George as the governor who could pardon him
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that perfectly captures the irony of the situation. Well-constructed and memorable.

jokes.text: My whole life has been a complete waste of time. And there's so much more to go.
jokes.setup: George reflecting on his life
jokes.punchline: The darkly comic realization that his wasted life is only going to continue
jokes.explanation: Perfect George line - captures his neurotic pessimism with dark humor. The 'so much more to go' is brilliantly depressing.

jokes.text: I broke up with her.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if George gave Patrice the tax papers
jokes.punchline: George's casual delivery of devastating news
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and understatement. The casual delivery makes the disaster even funnier.

jokes.text: [Jerry's stunned silence and reaction]
jokes.setup: George's casual announcement of the breakup
jokes.punchline: Jerry's visible shock and disbelief at the timing
jokes.explanation: Classic reaction beat. Jerry's silence speaks volumes about the magnitude of George's screw-up.

jokes.text: You're extremely... careful with money. I'm what? I'm cheap? You think I'm cheap?!
jokes.setup: George demanding Jerry tell him the truth
jokes.punchline: George's outraged reaction to Jerry's diplomatic way of calling him cheap
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - George can't handle the truth he demanded. Jerry's diplomatic phrasing makes George's reaction funnier.

jokes.text: When I was working, I spent, baby! Yeah, I know. Champagne, limos, cigars.
jokes.setup: George defending his spending habits
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic agreement listing George's 'extravagant' purchases
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Jerry's sarcasm undercuts George's self-aggrandizing. The items listed are perfectly George.

jokes.text: A pocketbook or a handbag? Is that relevant? She took them.
jokes.setup: George explaining where the papers are
jokes.punchline: Jerry's neurotic fixation on the exact terminology while missing the point
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry neurosis - focusing on irrelevant details when facing a crisis. Very in-character.

jokes.text: Well, wouldn't it be invisible? I mean, what, are you gonna just sense it's in front of the couch?
jokes.setup: Kramer planning to make a coffee table from a windshield
jokes.punchline: Jerry pointing out the obvious flaw in an invisible coffee table
jokes.explanation: Good logical comedy - pointing out the absurd impracticality of Kramer's idea with perfect reasoning.

jokes.text: I saw her naked. He saw me naked. Kramer saw me naked.
jokes.setup: The awkward revelation building
jokes.punchline: The repetitive, increasingly uncomfortable acknowledgment of the situation
jokes.explanation: The repetition builds the awkwardness effectively. Simple but well-executed cringe comedy.

jokes.text: All right, if it's gonna make you feel any better, you can see me naked. No, thank you. No, I want you to see me naked.
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to solve the awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly insistent offers making things more awkward
jokes.explanation: Elaine's logical but completely wrong solution escalates the discomfort perfectly.

jokes.text: As far as Mr. Johnson is concerned... that's another story.
jokes.setup: Jerry welcoming Kramer but setting boundaries
jokes.punchline: The euphemistic reference to male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent euphemistic humor with good delivery, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: You'll sense it.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out the coffee table will be invisible
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact response to an obviously flawed plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer deadpan delivery. The complete confidence in an absurd solution is quintessentially Kramer.

jokes.text: A mental institution.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking where Patrice is
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic reveal - the matter-of-fact delivery makes the shocking news even funnier. Great structure.

jokes.text: That's why I like to wear a belt when I'm naked. Because I feel it gives me something. Some, you know, 'I'm naked, but, you know...'
jokes.setup: Jerry's stand-up about being naked
jokes.punchline: The absurd logic of wearing a belt while naked for confidence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd observation. The psychological logic is hilariously flawed but somehow relatable.

jokes.text: To be naked and still be able to do this.
jokes.setup: Jerry talking about pockets on a belt
jokes.punchline: Jerry presumably doing the pocket gesture while naked with a belt
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback that extends the absurd premise logically.

jokes.text: I've driven women to lesbianism before... but never to a mental institution.
jokes.setup: George lamenting his effect on women
jokes.punchline: The escalation from lesbianism to mental institution as a worse outcome
jokes.explanation: Classic George line - self-deprecating with perfect escalation. The casual admission of past romantic disasters is perfectly in character and highly quotable.

jokes.text: My friend Bob Sacamano had shock treatments. But his synapses were so large, had no effect.
jokes.setup: Discussion of mental institution treatments
jokes.punchline: Kramer's completely incorrect medical explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer pseudo-scientific nonsense. The confident delivery of medical misinformation is very in-character.

jokes.text: Came over to my house for dinner, there was no soda... and he went berserk. He was screaming, 'Where's the Pepsi? Where's the Pepsi?'
jokes.setup: George talking about his cousin's mental issues
jokes.punchline: The trivial trigger and specific, repetitive demand for Pepsi
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal situation to breakdown. The Pepsi specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I should be in a place like this. I envy this woman. Get to wear slippers all day. Friends visit, they pity you. Pity's very underrated. I like it. It's good.
jokes.setup: George considering mental institution life
jokes.punchline: His twisted logic finding advantages in mental illness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly George - finding the silver lining in mental illness. The 'pity's underrated' is a brilliant observation.

jokes.text: Plus, they give you those word-association tests. I love those. They're great. There's no wrong answer. Potato. Tuberculosis. Blanket. Leroy. Grass. Tuberculosis.
jokes.setup: George's enthusiasm for psychological tests
jokes.punchline: His bizarre word associations, especially repeating 'tuberculosis'
jokes.explanation: Great George neurosis - his associations are hilariously wrong. The tuberculosis repetition suggests obsessive thinking.

jokes.text: [Kramer appears completely naked]
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about nudity and the naked coffee table
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual appearance in full nudity
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer physical comedy - the casual nudity is perfectly in character and creates maximum visual impact.

jokes.text: Listen, you want some leftovers? I made some African food. There's yambalas and sambusa.
jokes.setup: Kramer being completely naked
jokes.punchline: His casual offer of exotic food while nude, as if nothing's unusual
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer obliviousness - treating the bizarre situation as completely normal while offering exotic food.

jokes.text: It's invisible. So is everything cool or what?
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about the coffee table
jokes.punchline: Elaine's deadpan response and Kramer's casual check-in while naked
jokes.explanation: Good understatement and the casual 'everything cool' while naked is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: Who are you? This is my friend... Jerry. Why are you talking like that?
jokes.setup: Meeting Patrice at the mental institution
jokes.punchline: Patrice's altered mental state and George's awkward introduction
jokes.explanation: Effective setup for the mental institution visit. The change in Patrice's demeanor is believable and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Obviously, this isn't a mental institution.
jokes.setup: Jerry's earlier comment about mental institutions
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to take back his words
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Jerry's awkward backtracking makes the situation more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I'm incapable of guile. He's never guiled.
jokes.setup: George claiming he's incapable of guile
jokes.punchline: Jerry's backup using 'guile' as a verb
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay turning 'guile' into a verb. The mutual deception while claiming honesty is perfectly ironic.

jokes.text: There are no copies. Who makes copies?
jokes.setup: Patrice mentioning she needs copies
jokes.punchline: The devastating realization that they're completely screwed
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the disaster. George's bewildered 'who makes copies' shows how screwed they are.

jokes.text: It was 1987. I remember I talked to a guy, had, like, a maroon sport jacket... and he might have had a toupee. Oh, it was a weave?
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to track down a receipt
jokes.punchline: His specific but useless details about the salesperson's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how we remember irrelevant details. The toupee/weave distinction is funny.

jokes.text: I'm going to a poetry reading... with Patrice. First-time poets. It's in a burnt-out building down by the docks. It's supposed to be good.
jokes.setup: George's plans for the evening
jokes.punchline: The increasingly pretentious and depressing description of the poetry event
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - George getting sucked into pretentious activities. The setting description escalates beautifully.

jokes.text: You're going that way anyway! Come on.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying George has to chip in for the cab
jokes.punchline: George's classic cheap behavior trying to avoid paying
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the cheapness theme. George immediately proving Jerry's point about being careful with money.

jokes.text: At first, I thought, 'Well, IRS kind of sounds like Toys 'R' Us. Maybe it won't be so bad. Maybe they have a sense of fun about it.'
jokes.setup: Jerry's stand-up about being audited
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison between IRS and Toys 'R' Us based on similar lettering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational premise. The childish hope based on letter similarity is both absurd and relatable.

jokes.text: I think they should take all your receipts and put them... in one of those big, Lucite sweepstake drums... and just kind of crank it around there. Give you a feeling like you might win something.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting improvements to the audit process
jokes.punchline: The game show approach to tax auditing
jokes.explanation: Creative premise that transforms a stressful experience into a game show. The visual is vivid and amusing.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. That's another illegal deduction... but we do have some lovely parting gifts for you. Jail.
jokes.setup: The game show approach to auditing
jokes.punchline: The dark twist where the 'parting gift' is prison
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark punchline that completes the game show parody. The cheerful delivery of 'jail' is excellent.

tmdb_overview: George's relationship with a former IRS worker eases Jerry's tax worries--until she becomes George's former relationship; Kramer sees too much of Elaine while seeing her roommate.
